channel.title: Air Fryer Recipes on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Air Fryer Recipes on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Air Fryer Panko Chicken Thighs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs. Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs (approximately 4 thighs)
  • 2 large Eggs
  • 2/3 cup All Purpose Flour
  • 2/3 cup Cornstarch
  • 1 cup Panko Breadcrumbs
  • 1 teaspoon Paprika
  • 1 teaspoon Salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon Pepper
  • 1/2 tablespoon Garlic Powder
  • 1/2 tablespoon Onion Powder

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, mix flour, cornstarch, and spices together.
  2. In a separate small bowl, whisk eggs.
  3. In a third bowl large enough to dip a whole chicken thigh, pour in the Panko bread crumbs.
  4. Coat a piece of chicken in flour mixture, then dredge in the egg wash, and finish coating it with the panko crumbs. Place on a clean solid surface, such as a plate or cutting board.
  5. Repeat for each piece of chicken.
  6. Pre-heat the air fryer to 350 degrees F. (1-2 minutes depending on your air fryer model).
  7. Spray your air fryer basked and chicken thighs with olive oil spray. The main reason for nonstick cooking spray is it will help the chicken thighs crisp up more and keep them from sticking to the basket.
  8. Working in batches, place the thighs in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
  9. Air fry the chicken on 350F for 12 minutes. Use tongs to flip chicken and air fry for another 3-5 minutes to make sure all sides of the chicken are nice and crisp. Check the chicken with a meat thermometer to make sure the internal temperature reaches 165F.

Credit for this recipe goes to TopRockin.com:

item.title: Air Fryer Chicken Thighs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5-6 boneless skinless chicken thighs patted dry
  • 1 teaspoongarlic powder
  • 1 teaspoonsmoked paprika
  • ½ teaspoonoregano
  • ½ teaspoonsalt
  • ¼ teaspoonblack pepper

Instructions

  1. Pat chicken thighs dry with a paper towel, then transfer to a bowl.
  2. Combine all the seasonings together in a bowl, then pour them into the bowl with the chicken and use your hands to coat the chicken with the spices.
  3. Preheat your air fryer to 380° (mine took 3 minutes to preheat). Then, place chicken smooth side down into the air fryer, spray with nonstick spray/olive oil spray, and cook at 380° for 12 minutes. NOTE: every air fryer is different, so cook times may vary.
  4. Once the air fryer beeps, use kitchen tongs to flip the chicken over, and cook for an additional 6-8 minutes on the second side, or until chicken is cooked through.  (Billy’s note:  I only put them in for 4 additional mins instead of 6-8.  I think they’d have been too dry with that, so gauge them when you flip them.)

Credit for this recipe to goes to The Almond Eater:

item.title: Air Fryer Mashed Potato Balls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups mashed potatoes (preferably made with butter and cream)
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 cup panko breadcrumbs
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder (optional)
  • 1/2 teaspoon onion powder (optional)
  • Olive oil spray (or any cooking spray)

Instructions

  1. Prepare the Mashed Potatoes:If you haven’t already, prepare mashed potatoes. Ensure they are smooth and slightly on the thicker side (not too runny).
  2. Mix the Ingredients:In a large bowl, combine the mashed potatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder (if using). Stir until the cheese is evenly distributed.
  3. Form the Balls:Scoop about 1 tablespoon of the mashed potato mixture and roll it into a ball. Continue until all the mixture is used. You should be able to form 12–16 balls, depending on their size.
  4. Coat the Balls:Set up a breading station:In one shallow dish, place the flour.In another dish, beat the egg.In a third dish, add the panko breadcrumbs.Roll each mashed potato ball first in the flour, then dip in the egg, and finally coat it in the panko breadcrumbs. Press gently to ensure the breadcrumbs stick well.
  5. Preheat the Air Fryer:Preheat your air fryer to 375°F (190°C) for about 3-5 minutes.
  6. Air Fry the Mashed Potato Balls:Lightly spray the air fryer basket with olive oil.Place the potato balls in the air fryer basket, leaving some space between them for air circulation.Lightly spray the tops of the balls with more olive oil.Air fry at 375°F for 8-10 minutes or until they are golden brown and crispy, flipping them halfway through the cooking time for even crispiness.
  7. Serve:Once golden and crispy, remove the mashed potato balls from the air fryer. Let them cool slightly before serving.You can serve these with a dipping sauce, such as sour cream, ranch, or ketchup!

Tips:
